concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in its left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in its right subtree are greater.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a tree structure, typically containing data and pointers to child nodes.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a tree, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Leaf Node
concepts.short_definition: A node with no children.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has one or more child nodes.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that is directly connected to another node (its parent).

concepts.name: Left Child
concepts.short_definition: The child node of a parent node that is located to its left.

concepts.name: Right Child
concepts.short_definition: The child node of a parent node that is located to its right.

concepts.name: Key
concepts.short_definition: The information stored within a node of the BST.

concepts.name: BST Property
concepts.short_definition: The rule that dictates the arrangement of keys in a BST: left subtree keys < node key < right subtree keys.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new key into a BST while maintaining the BST property.

concepts.name: Search
concepts.short_definition: The process of locating a specific key within a BST.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The process of removing a key from a BST while maintaining the BST property.

concepts.name: Minimum Value
concepts.short_definition: The smallest key present in a BST, found by traversing left as far as possible from the root.

concepts.name: Maximum Value
concepts.short_definition: The largest key present in a BST, found by traversing right as far as possible from the root.

concepts.name: In-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A tree traversal method that visits nodes in ascending order of their keys.

concepts.name: Pre-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A tree traversal method that visits the root node first, then its left subtree, then its right subtree.

concepts.name: Post-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A tree traversal method that visits the left subtree, then the right subtree, then the root node.

concepts.name: Height
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges on the longest path from the root to a leaf node.

concepts.name: Depth
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges from the root to a specific node.

concepts.name: Balance
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how evenly distributed the nodes are in a tree, affecting performance.

concepts.name: Unbalanced BST
concepts.short_definition: A BST where the height difference between left and right subtrees is significant, leading to poor performance.

concepts.name: Balanced BST
concepts.short_definition: A BST that maintains a certain level of balance to ensure efficient operations.

concepts.name: Successor
concepts.short_definition: The node with the smallest key that is greater than a given node's key.

concepts.name: Predecessor
concepts.short_definition: The node with the largest key that is smaller than a given node's key.

concepts.name: Recursive Insertion
concepts.short_definition: Inserting a node into a BST using a recursive approach.

concepts.name: Iterative Insertion
concepts.short_definition: Inserting a node into a BST using an iterative approach.

concepts.name: Recursive Search
concepts.short_definition: Searching for a key in a BST using a recursive approach.

concepts.name: Iterative Search
concepts.short_definition: Searching for a key in a BST using an iterative approach.

concepts.name: Deletion Case 1
concepts.short_definition: Deleting a node with no children.

concepts.name: Deletion Case 2
concepts.short_definition: Deleting a node with exactly one child.

concepts.name: Deletion Case 3
concepts.short_definition: Deleting a node with two children.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the efficiency of BST operations in terms of input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the memory required by a BST.

concepts.name: Average Case
concepts.short_definition: The expected performance of BST operations on a randomly built tree.

concepts.name: Worst Case
concepts.short_definition: The performance of BST operations on a highly unbalanced tree.

concepts.name: Tree Visualization
concepts.short_definition: Representing a BST graphically to understand its structure.

concepts.name: Applications of BST
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and problems where BSTs are effectively used.
